response.summary: The full health plan screen is structurally present, but key visible copy is not semantically fit for end users. It uses internal/product jargon like “active profile” and “generated,” does not clearly surface the intended next action on the screen itself, and leans on mechanics-oriented wording instead of user value.

findings.observed: The screen heading and related labels use internal-facing terms such as “Every preventive step for your active profile” and “All generated preventive items.” This does not read like natural end-user language and does not clearly tell the user whose plan they are viewing in plain terms.
